ogTitle: Casterton vs Castle Donnington: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Casterton and Castle Donnington.
heading: Casterton vs Castle Donnington — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Casterton, Victoria and Castle Donnington, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Casterton has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Castle Donnington (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Casterton or Castle Donnington safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Casterton has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Castle Donnington scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Casterton scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Castle Donnington scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Casterton or Castle Donnington?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Casterton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
